text: Court is in session for Case No. MT-2026-08. Mr. Prosecutor, call your first witness.
I call Mr. Kamal Gunawardena to the stand. Mr. Gunawardena, please describe what happened on the night of January 5th at your residence in Nugegoda.
I was sleeping when I heard a loud crash near the kitchen window. I went to investigate and saw the defendant, Arunasalam, standing there with a large knife.
Did the defendant threaten you with this knife?
Yes, he pointed it at my chest and demanded my wallet and my wife's jewelry. He said if I moved, he would kill me.
And did you hand over the items?
I had no choice. I gave him two gold rings and 50,000 rupees. He then fled through the same window he entered.
This seems like a clear case of robbery under Section 380 of the Penal Code. Defense, do you wish to cross-examine?
Mr. Gunawardena, was the room well-lit? In your initial statement, you said the lights were out.
The kitchen lights were off, but the street light from outside was shining directly on his face. I am 100% sure it was him.
But isn't it true you've had a personal dispute with Arunasalam over a land boundary for years?
That is irrelevant! He robbed me at knife-point!
